mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate is above 2%.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 2% do not have lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods below 2%, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Very low unemployment combined with continued payroll expansion signals late-cycle labor tightness, which increases wage-pressure expectations and monetary tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 4.0 and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the interaction term between low UNRATE and PAYEMS growth has a non-negative coefficient in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is not followed by above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the estimated return spread between high and low RSAFS-growth terciles is less than zero.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in industrial production signals weakening real output and profit-cycle deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice cyclical earnings.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing events are followed by non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if hit-rate for negative 5-day returns is below 50% in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts during a rising-rate environment signal rate-sensitive demand destruction and future weakness in construction-linked activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 6 months while FEDFUNDS rises more than 50 basis points over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-decline and FEDFUNDS-rise trigger does not produce lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than either condition alone, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI inflation with a federal funds rate below the inflation run-rate signals insufficient monetary restraint and future policy repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 4.0 and FEDFUNDS is below that CPI growth rate
regime_hint: inflation scare with policy catch-up risk
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have average 20-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average, or if CPI acceleration conditional on low FEDFUNDS has a positive return coefficient in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth reflects broad demand expansion and stronger aggregate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than quarters with negative GDPC1 growth, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized improvement in industrial production and retail sales signals simultaneous supply-side output momentum and demand absorption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS each cross above their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint upside crossings in INDPRO and RSAFS do not produce higher 5-day S&P 500 returns than single-series crossings, or if the joint signal hit-rate for positive returns is below 50%.
